title: Devouring the Property of Orphans
subtitle: Consuming Fire into One's Own Belly

verses.arabic: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ يَأْكُلُونَ أَمْوَالَ الْيَتَامَىٰ ظُلْمًا إِنَّمَا يَأْكُلُونَ فِي بُطُونِهِمْ نَارًا ۖ وَسَيَصْلَوْنَ سَعِيرًا
verses.translation: Indeed, those who devour the property of orphans unjustly are only consuming into their bellies fire. And they will be burned in a Blaze.
No! But you do not honor the orphan.

verses.arabic: وَابْتَلُوا الْيَتَامَىٰ حَتَّىٰ إِذَا بَلَغُوا النِّكَاحَ فَإِنْ آنَسْتُم مِّنْهُمْ رُشْدًا فَادْفَعُوا إِلَيْهِمْ أَمْوَالَهُمْ
verses.translation: And test the orphans [in their abilities] until they reach marriageable age. Then if you perceive in them sound judgment, release their property to them.

brief_explanation: The Quran delivers one of the most visceral warnings in all of scripture to those who consume orphan wealth: they are literally eating fire into their own bellies and will burn in the Blaze (Sa'ir). The orphan is among the most vulnerable members of society — without a father to protect their rights — and consuming their wealth is framed in the Quran as an act of pure zulm (injustice) against the most defenceless. Allah honours those who guard and develop orphan wealth until the orphan comes of age, instructing guardians in detail how to manage it. The Quran warns in Surah Al-Fajr that a civilization's moral worth is measured by how it treats its orphans, and condemned are those who fail to honour them.
capital_punishment: There is no hadd execution specifically for devouring orphan wealth. It is treated as a major sin requiring immediate cessation, full restitution of the property taken, and repentance. Under Islamic civil law, the guardian is legally accountable for orphan wealth they hold in trust, and Islamic courts require them to return it in full with no reduction. The state may impose ta'zir (imprisonment, fines) on guardians who misappropriate orphan funds. If the act also constitutes theft or fraud, the relevant penalties of those crimes may apply.
